seo.title: Building Voice Agents with Haystack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with Haystack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with Haystack enables real-time conversational AI pipelines that process speech-to-text, reason with LLMs, and generate text-to-speech responses. Guardrails are essential due to the high-stakes nature of voice interactions, where latency must stay sub-second while ensuring response safety, coherence, and factual accuracy. Vex integration provides runtime verification to catch hallucinations, irrelevance, and safety violations before they reach users.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with Haystack
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents in Haystack follow a modular pipeline architecture optimized for low latency. Audio input is transcribed via a speech-to-text component (e.g., Whisper), feeding into a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline with vector retrievers and LLM generators. The output passes through safety filters before text-to-speech synthesis (e.g., ElevenLabs or Piper). Custom components handle session state for conversation coherence.

The pipeline enforces strict latency budgets: STT <200ms, retrieval <100ms, generation <400ms, TTS <200ms. Haystack's async execution and connection pooling ensure sub-second end-to-end latency. Vex slots in as a verification component post-generation to validate outputs against multiple criteria before TTS.

Key to reliability is pipeline orchestration via Haystack's Pipeline class, with streaming support for partial outputs and error recovery mechanisms.

risks.name: Pipeline component output type mismatches
risks.description: STT returns audio chunks instead of text, breaking retriever input expectations
risks.mitigation: Vex type validation component enforces Document/List[Document] contracts

risks.name: Retriever returning irrelevant documents
risks.description: Voice queries retrieve off-topic docs due to acoustic noise or query ambiguity
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic relevance scoring with cosine threshold >0.75

risks.name: Generator hallucinating beyond retrieved context
risks.description: LLM fabricates medical/legal advice outside RAG context in voice responses
risks.mitigation: Vex context faithfulness verification using entailment models

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: Retriever or generator exceeds 500ms, breaking real-time voice interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex timeout enforcement with fallback responses

implementation.title: Basic Voice Agent Pipeline
implementation.description: Core Haystack pipeline with STT → RAG → TTS. Missing safety checks.
implementation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator
from haystack.utils import ComponentDevice

# Voice pipeline without guardrails
voice_pipeline = Pipeline()
voice_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(document_store))
voice_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIGenerator(api_key="os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY')"))
voice_pipeline.connect("retriever", "generator")

implementation.title: Streaming Response for Low Latency
implementation.description: Async streaming ensures sub-second perceived latency for voice UX.
implementation.code:
import asyncio
from haystack import Pipeline

async def stream_voice_response(audio_text: str):
    result = await voice_pipeline.arun(
        query=audio_text,
        generate_kwargs={"stream": True}
    )
    async for token in result["generation"]["streams"]:
        yield token  # Stream to TTS incrementally

implementation.title: Conversation State Management
implementation.description: Maintains context across voice turns using session store.
implementation.code:
from haystack.components.builders import ChatPromptBuilder

prompt_builder = ChatPromptBuilder(
    template="""Conversation History:\n{{ history }}\nUser: {{ query }}\nAssistant:""",
    history_template="{{ messages }}"
)
voice_pipeline.add_component("prompt_builder", prompt_builder)
voice_pipeline.connect("chat_history", "prompt_builder")

implementation.title: Latency Monitoring Wrapper
implementation.description: Wraps components to enforce real-time constraints.
implementation.code:
import time
from haystack import Pipeline

class LatencyMonitor:
    def __init__(self, max_latency=0.5):
        self.max_latency = max_latency
    
    async def __call__(self, **kwargs):
        start = time.time()
        result = await self.wrapped_component(**kwargs)
        if time.time() - start > self.max_latency:
            raise TimeoutError("Latency budget exceeded")
        return result

vexIntegration.title: Vex Custom Verification Component
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as pipeline component for multi-check verification.

vexIntegration.title: Pipeline with Vex Integration
vexIntegration.description: Full pipeline inserting Vex post-generation, pre-TTS.
vexIntegration.code:
voice_pipeline = Pipeline()
voice_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever(store))
voice_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIGenerator())
voice_pipeline.add_component("vex", VexVerifier())
voice_pipeline.add_component("tts", TextToSpeech())

voice_pipeline.connect("retriever", "generator")
voice_pipeline.connect("generator", "vex")
voice_pipeline.connect("vex.verified", "tts.text")

# Run with safety
result = voice_pipeline.run(query=transcribed_speech)

vexIntegration.title: Real-time Latency + Safety Checks
vexIntegration.description: Vex enforces both functional and non-functional requirements.

cta.heading: Secure Your Haystack Voice Agents
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your voice pipelines today for production-grade reliability.
